title: pilosella onegensis subsp. madara
intro: Pilosella onegensis subsp. madara is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Pilosella genus, commonly known as the madara buttercup or madara hawk's beard, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Pilosella onegensis subsp. madara include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as an infusion or tincture, although its limited availability and potential toxicity require careful handling and consultation with a medical professional.
